title: prismatomeris fragrans subsp. andamanica
intro: Prismatomeris fragrans subsp. andamanica, also known as Andaman fragrant prismatomeris, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, and is believed to possess antioxidant and cytotoxic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its ability to exhibit anti-cancer and anti-diabetic activities. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health ailments.
